name: AWS Chime SDK
what_it_does: AWS managed service providing real-time audio, video, screen sharing, and messaging primitives via SDKs for embedding communication features into custom applications.
use_cases: Building telehealth platforms with HIPAA-compliant video consultations
Embedding virtual classroom functionality with up to 250 attendees into edtech applications
Creating AI-orchestrated meeting workflows: auto-creating meetings, inviting participants, and starting transcriptions via API
Customer service applications with agent-controlled audio/video routing and PSTN dial-in support
not_for: Teams wanting a prebuilt video conferencing product — Chime SDK is primitives-only and requires substantial client-side development
Startups needing simple embed-and-go video without AWS infrastructure expertise
Use cases requiring more than 250 meeting attendees (use Amazon IVS for broadcast-scale)
best_when: Best when you are already in the AWS ecosystem, need HIPAA/compliance-grade real-time communications, and have engineering capacity to build a custom meeting experience on top of managed media infrastructure.
avoid_when: Avoid when you want a turnkey video solution, have no AWS expertise, or when the pay-per-minute pricing model makes costs unpredictable for your use case.

auth.notes: Authentication uses AWS IAM/STS — requires IAM roles, policies, and either long-lived access keys or assumed-role tokens. Meeting attendees receive short-lived join tokens generated server-side. Significantly more complex than API-key based services; agents must handle AWS credential management (SDK credential chain, environment vars, or instance profiles).

pricing.paid_tiers: Audio: $0.0017 per attendee-minute. Video: $0.0170 per attendee-minute. Screen share: $0.0170 per attendee-minute. PSTN dial-in: varies by country (~$0.002–$0.006/min). Amazon Transcribe integration billed separately. No minimum commitment.
pricing.notes: AWS account required with billing enabled. Costs can compound quickly for video-heavy applications — a 60-minute video meeting with 10 attendees costs approximately $10.20 in video fees alone.

performance.rate_limits: CreateMeeting: 20 TPS per account. CreateAttendee: 20 TPS. DeleteMeeting: 20 TPS. Service limits adjustable via AWS Support. Chime SDK Messaging has separate limits (100 TPS for SendChannelMessage).

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: AWS SDK errors include structured error codes (e.g., NotFoundException, ForbiddenException) with HTTP status codes and request IDs for tracing. Error messages are generally clear but AWS IAM policy errors can be cryptic for agents to interpret.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: CreateMeeting supports a ClientRequestToken for idempotency. CreateAttendee does not have a native idempotency key — retries may create duplicate attendees.
known_agent_gotchas: Meetings auto-delete after all attendees leave or after a 5-minute idle period — agents must recreate meetings if they expire
Meeting regions must be specified at creation time and cannot be changed — agents need region-selection logic for global use
Server-side token generation for attendees requires a backend service; agents cannot call the API directly from client-side without exposing IAM credentials
IAM credential management is significantly more complex than simple API keys — agents need AWS SDK credential chain support
PSTN integration (Amazon Chime Voice Connector) is a separate product with its own provisioning process

security_score_components.security_notes: AWS IAM provides industry-leading auth with fine-grained policy control, MFA, and temporary credentials via STS. HIPAA BAA available. Meetings are encrypted in transit (SRTP/DTLS) and media is not stored by default.
